lines.raw: Finding fossil man | 寻找化石人

lines.english: Why are legends handed down by storytellers useful?
lines.chinese: 说书人世代相传的传说为什么有用？
lines.raw: Why are legends handed down by storytellers useful? | 说书人世代相传的传说为什么有用？

lines.english: We can read of things that happened 5, 000 years ago in the Near East,
lines.chinese: 我们能读到5000年前近东发生的事，
lines.raw: We can read of things that happened 5, 000 years ago in the Near East, | 我们能读到5000年前近东发生的事，

lines.english: where people first learned to write.
lines.chinese: 那里的人们最早学会了书写。
lines.raw: where people first learned to write. | 那里的人们最早学会了书写。

lines.english: But there are some parts of the world where even now people cannot write.
lines.chinese: 但世界上的某些地方，即使到现在，人们仍然不会书写。
lines.raw: But there are some parts of the world where even now people cannot write. | 但世界上的某些地方，即使到现在，人们仍然不会书写。

lines.english: The only way that they can preserve their history is to recount it as sagas legends handed down from one generation of storytellers to another.
lines.chinese: 他们保存历史的唯一方法，就是把一代代说书人流传下来的史诗故事讲述出来。
lines.raw: The only way that they can preserve their history is to recount it as sagas legends handed down from one generation of storytellers to another. | 他们保存历史的唯一方法，就是把一代代说书人流传下来的史诗故事讲述出来。

lines.english: These legends are useful because they can tell us something about migrations of people who lived long ago,
lines.chinese: 这些传说很有用，因为它们能告诉我们一些关于远古人类迁徙的信息，
lines.raw: These legends are useful because they can tell us something about migrations of people who lived long ago, | 这些传说很有用，因为它们能告诉我们一些关于远古人类迁徙的信息，

lines.english: but none could write down what they did.
lines.chinese: 但没有人能记录下他们的所作所为。
lines.raw: but none could write down what they did. | 但没有人能记录下他们的所作所为。

lines.english: Anthropologists wondered where the remote ancestors of the Polynesian peoples now living in the Pacific Islandscame from.
lines.chinese: 人类学家们想知道，如今居住在太平洋岛屿上的波利尼西亚人的远古祖先来自何方。
lines.raw: Anthropologists wondered where the remote ancestors of the Polynesian peoples now living in the Pacific Islandscame from. | 人类学家们想知道，如今居住在太平洋岛屿上的波利尼西亚人的远古祖先来自何方。

lines.english: The sagas of these people explain that some of them came from Indonesia about 2, 000 years ago.
lines.chinese: 这些人的史诗记载，他们中的一部分人大约在2000年前从印度尼西亚迁徙而来。
lines.raw: The sagas of these people explain that some of them came from Indonesia about 2, 000 years ago. | 这些人的史诗记载，他们中的一部分人大约在2000年前从印度尼西亚迁徙而来。

lines.english: But the first people who were like ourselves lived so long ago that even their sagas, if they had any, are forgotten.
lines.chinese: 但是，第一批与我们相似的人类生活在如此久远的年代，以至于即使他们曾有史诗，也早已被遗忘。
lines.raw: But the first people who were like ourselves lived so long ago that even their sagas, if they had any, are forgotten. | 但是，第一批与我们相似的人类生活在如此久远的年代，以至于即使他们曾有史诗，也早已被遗忘。

lines.english: So archaeologists have neither history nor legends to help them to find out where the first 'modern men' came from.
lines.chinese: 因此，考古学家既没有历史记载，也没有传说来帮助他们找出第一批“现代人”的起源。
lines.raw: So archaeologists have neither history nor legends to help them to find out where the first 'modern men' came from. | 因此，考古学家既没有历史记载，也没有传说来帮助他们找出第一批“现代人”的起源。

lines.english: Fortunately, however, ancient men made tools of stone, especially flint,
lines.chinese: 然而，幸运的是，远古人类制造了石器，尤其是燧石工具，
lines.raw: Fortunately, however, ancient men made tools of stone, especially flint, | 然而，幸运的是，远古人类制造了石器，尤其是燧石工具，

lines.english: because this is easier to shape than other kinds.
lines.chinese: 因为它比其他种类的材料更容易塑形。
lines.raw: because this is easier to shape than other kinds. | 因为它比其他种类的材料更容易塑形。

lines.english: They may also have used wood and skins, but these have rotted away.
lines.chinese: 他们也可能使用过木头和兽皮，但这些都已经腐烂殆尽了。
lines.raw: They may also have used wood and skins, but these have rotted away. | 他们也可能使用过木头和兽皮，但这些都已经腐烂殆尽了。

lines.english: Stone does not decay, and so the tools of long ago have remained when even the bones of the men who made them have disappeared without trace.
lines.chinese: 石头不会腐烂，因此，即使制造这些工具的人的骨骸早已消失得无影无踪，远古的石器工具依然留存至今。
lines.raw: Stone does not decay, and so the tools of long ago have remained when even the bones of the men who made them have disappeared without trace. | 石头不会腐烂，因此，即使制造这些工具的人的骨骸早已消失得无影无踪，远古的石器工具依然留存至今。
